revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:半家]]
'''Hanke''' (半家) refers to a Rank of {{ill|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|Kugyō=Kugyō|lt=noble families|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3138036}} (Kugyō) that was established from the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}} onward. Among the {{ill|dōjō-ke|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dōjō-ke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11427847}}, it represents the lowest tier of the {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=aristocracy|1=Nobility}} and includes families of {{ill|Minamoto clan|en|Minamoto clan|qid=Q599881|lt=Minamoto clan|1=Minamoto clan}}, {{ill|Taira clan|en|Taira clan|qid=Q1079102|lt=Taira clan|1=Taira clan}}, {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|qid=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}}, and {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|qid=Q1500519|lt=Tachibana clan|1=Tachibana clan (kuge)}} descent, known for their specialized skills in serving the {{ill|Royal court|en|Royal court|qid=Q1134237|lt=Imperial Court of Japan|1=Royal court}}.
== Overview == In terms of {{ill|court Rank|ja|官位|1=East Asian government service ranking|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Kan'i system|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11452076}}, Hanke families were promoted in a manner similar to {{ill|Harima-ke|ja|羽林家|1=Baronial Houses|1=Baronial Houses|2=ja|3=羽林家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harima-ke|qq=}} or {{ill|Mei families|ja|名家 (公家)|1=Mei families|2=ja|3=名家 (公家)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mei families|qq=}}, with some families rising to the position of {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=dainagon|1=Dainagon}} without passing through {{ill|Konoefu|ja|近衛府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konoefu|1=Konoefu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11638974}} or {{ill|benkan positions|ja|弁官|1=benkan|lt=Benkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/benkan positions|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3270768}}. However, even as {{ill|kugyō|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3138036}}, most remained {{ill|Hisanagi|lt=Hisanagi|lt=Non-councilors|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/non-councilors|qq=}} (非参議).
After the implementation of the {{ill|Kazoku Act|ja|華族令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazoku Act|1=Peerage Law|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620084}}, all Hanke families were granted the title of {{ill|Viscount|en|Viscount|qid=Q185902|lt=viscount|1=Viscount}}, although the {{ill|Sawa family|ja|澤家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sawa family|1=Sawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11565861}} was later promoted to {{ill|Count|en|Count|qid=Q3519259|lt=count|1=Count}} for their contributions during the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}.
== List of Hanke Families == === Fujiwara Clan (2 families) === * {{ill|Takakura family|ja|高倉家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takakura family|qq=}}: A branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|qid=Q2975268|lt=Fujiwara Hokke|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}} descended from {{ill|Fujiwara no Nagara|en|Fujiwara no Nagara|qid=Q667119|lt=Fujiwara no Nagara|1=Fujiwara no Nagara}}, son of {{ill|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|en|Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|qid=Q3090616|lt=Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu|1=Fujiwara no Fuyutsugu}}, through {{ill|Fujiwara no Eiki|ja|藤原永季|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Eiki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An {{ill|old family|ja|旧家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/old family|1=Old Houses|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11511033}}. Their family occupation included {{ill|court etiquette|ja|有職故実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court etiquette|1=Yūsoku Kojitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1623327}} and {{ill|court costumes|ja|装束|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court costumes|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 衣装|1=Costume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9053464}}. * {{ill|Tomiokōji family|ja|富小路家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiokōji family|1=Tominokouji Family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11456292}}: Also referred to as a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara Hok-ke|en|Fujiwara Hok-ke|qid=Q2975268|lt=Fujiwara Hokke|1=Fujiwara Hok-ke}}, specifically the {{ill|Kujō lineage|ja|九条流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujō lineage|1=Kujo school|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11370406}} under the {{ill|Nijō family|en|Nijō family|qid=Q3065387|lt=Nijō family|1=Nijō family}}. Possibly descended from {{ill|Nijō Michihira|en|Nijō Michihira|qid=Q3341478|lt=Nijō Michihira|1=Nijō Michihira}}'s second son, {{ill|Tomiokōji Michinao|ja|富小路道直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomiokōji Michinao|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. An old family. Their family occupation included {{ill|Waka (poetry)|en|Waka (poetry)|qid=Q737148|lt=waka poetry|1=Waka (poetry)}} and {{ill|Haikai|en|Haikai|qid=Q17049321|lt=haikai|1=Haikai}}.
=== Seiwa Genji (1 family) === * {{ill|Takeuchi family|ja|竹内家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi family|1=Takenouchi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11598864}}: A branch of the {{ill|Seiwa Genji|en|Seiwa Genji|qid=Q2164991|lt=Seiwa Genji|1=Seiwa Genji}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|en|Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|qid=Q3083118|lt=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu|1=Minamoto no Yoshimitsu}}, whose fourth son, {{ill|Minamoto no Moriyoshi|ja|源盛義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Moriyoshi|1=Minamoto no Moriyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564234}}, founded the family. The founder was {{ill|Takeuchi Tokiharu|ja|竹内季治|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeuchi Tokiharu|1=takenouchi sueharu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q102246540}}. An old family. Their family occupation included {{ill|archery|ja|弓箭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/archery|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弓矢|1=Bow and arrow|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q19827042}}, {{ill|Shō (instrument)|en|Shō (instrument)|qid=Q1975355|lt=shō flute|1=Shō (instrument)}}, and waka poetry.
=== Uda Genji (2 families) === * {{ill|Itsutsuji family|ja|五辻家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutsuji family|1=Itsutsuji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372684}}: A branch of the {{ill|Uda Genji|en|Uda Genji|qid=Q4846201|lt=Uda Genji|1=Uda Genji}}, descended from {{ill|Minamoto no Masanobu|en|Minamoto no Masanobu|qid=Q3314711|lt=Minamoto no Masanobu|1=Minamoto no Masanobu}}, with his sixth-generation descendant {{ill|Minamoto no Nakanaka|ja|源仲兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Nakanaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} as the founder. An old family. Their family occupation included {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=kagura|1=Kagura}}. * {{ill|Jikōji family|ja|慈光寺家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikōji family|1=Jikōji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11494986}}: A cadet branch of the Itsutsuji family. Founded by {{ill|Jikōji Nakakiyo|ja|慈光寺仲清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikōji Nakakiyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the son of {{ill|Itsutsuji Tokitsura|ja|五辻遠兼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsutsuji Tokitsura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. After the family line ended in the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q117415390}}, it was revived in the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} by {{ill|Jikōji Fuyunaka|ja|慈光寺冬仲|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jikōji Fuyunaka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A {{ill|new family|ja|新家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/new family|1=shinke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11501762}}.
=== Kazan Genji (1 family) === * {{ill|Shirakawa Hakuō family|ja|白川伯王家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shirakawa Hakuō family|1=Shirakawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579697}}: A branch of the {{ill|Kazan Genji|ja|花山源氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazan Genji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白川伯王家|1=Shirakawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579697}}, descended from {{ill|Prince Kiyohito|ja|清仁親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kiyohito|1=Kiyohito-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17502448}}. Founded by {{ill|Minamoto no Akiyasu|ja|源顕康|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Akiyasu|1=minamoto no akiyasu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q118746998}}. An old family. They inherited the title of {{ill|Jingihaku|ja|神祇伯|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingihaku|1=Minister of Divinities|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17210025}} and were referred to as the {{ill|Haku family|ja|伯家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haku family|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 白川伯王家|1=Shirakawa family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579697}}.
=== Kanmu Heishi (2 families) === * {{ill|Nishinotōin family|ja|西洞院家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin family|1=Nishinotoin family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11629027}}: A branch of the {{ill|Kanmu Heishi|ja|桓武平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanmu Heishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平氏|1=Taira clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1079102}}, descended from {{ill|Takamune Ō|ja|高棟王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamune Ō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 平高棟|1=Taira no Takamune|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11483792}}. Founded by {{ill|Nishinotōin Yukitoki|ja|西洞院行時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishinotōin Yukitoki|1=Nishinotōin Yukitoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q83207835}}. An old family. * {{ill|Ishii family|ja|石井家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii family|1=Ishii family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584926}}: A cadet branch of the Nishinotōin family. Founded by {{ill|Ishii Yukitoyo|ja|石井行豊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii Yukitoyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the son of {{ill|Hiramatsu Tokikazu|ja|平松時量|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiramatsu Tokikazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A new family.
=== Sugawara Clan (6 families) === * {{ill|Takatsuji family|ja|高辻家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji family|1=Takatsuji family|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11672888}}: A branch of the {{ill|Sugawara clan|en|Sugawara clan|qid=Q1771125|lt=Sugawara clan|1=Sugawara clan}}, descended from {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q382005}} through his son {{ill|Sugawara no Sadayoshi|ja|菅原定義|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Sadayoshi|1=Sugawara no Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11619174}}. Founded by {{ill|Takatsuji Koretsuna|ja|高辻是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菅原是綱|1=Sugawara no Koretsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q118746916}}. An old family. Their family occupation included {{ill|kidendō|ja|紀伝道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kidendō|1=Kidendō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11604971}}, and they inherited the title of {{ill|monjō hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/monjō hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500074}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|ja|Kugyō|1=Kugyō|2=ja|3=公卿|1=Kugyō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3138036}} * {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|qid=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} * {{ill|Nobility|en|Nobility|qid=Q134737|lt=Aristocracy|1=Nobility}} * {{ill|Heian aristocracy|ja|平安貴族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian aristocracy|1=Heian aristocracy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11482408}} * {{ill|Hisansangi|ja|非参議|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisansangi|1=Hisanagi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11663700}} * {{ill|Dōjō-ke|ja|堂上家|1=Tōshōke|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dōjō-ke|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11427847}}
{{japanese-history-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|半家 (公家)|qq=Half-house (Kuge)|qq=ja|qq=半家 (公家)|version=97911090|comment=Imported full ja history}}qq=}}
{{draft categories| [[ko:반가]][[zh:半家]] |qq=}}
<!--Half-house (Kuge)--><!--Half-house (Kuge)--> {{Tōshōke families|qq=}} <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:半家 (公家)]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[ja:半家 (公家)]] {{wikidata link|Q11406995}} [[Category:Court Noble Families]] [[Category:Half-house (Kuge)]] [[Category:Kuge]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]]
